metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reconcile the plan's stated source-sync mechanism (rsync from mirror clone at MIRROR_DIR) with the committed autodeploy.sh (which uses `git checkout` in DEPLOY_REPO with no rsync/MIRROR_DIR): either update autodeploy.sh to implement the mirror-clone+rsync approach described in Box-adaptation §2, or update the plan to accurately reflect a git-checkout-based approach that is compatible with the observed box state (no .git in /opt/rebar).
findings.finding: The plan's Box-adaptation section asserts the source-sync mechanism is `rsync -a --delete $MIRROR_DIR/ /opt/rebar/` from a self-bootstrapped regular mirror clone at MIRROR_DIR=/var/lib/rebar/mirror, but the committed autodeploy.sh contains NO rsync, NO MIRROR_DIR variable, and instead performs `git checkout -q "$TARGET"` inside DEPLOY_REPO. The box observation explicitly states `/opt/rebar` is 'a plain copy — NOT a git checkout (no .git)', meaning the `git checkout` call will fail in the live environment (no .git to operate against). This is an unverified capability assertion about the core source-sync mechanism.
findings.location: Plan §Box-adaptation > 'Adapted mechanism' item 2 (rsync) vs autodeploy.sh lines 59, 97, 102, 130, 138
findings.suggested_fix: Implement the mirror-clone+rsync approach the plan describes: add MIRROR_DIR=/var/lib/rebar/mirror, bootstrap via `git clone https://github.com/navapbc/rebar.git $MIRROR_DIR` if absent, run fetch+rev-parse from $MIRROR_DIR, then `rsync -a --delete --exclude '/.git' --exclude 'infra/compose/.env' $MIRROR_DIR/ /opt/rebar/`. All git ops (fetch, rev-parse, diff) run in MIRROR_DIR; /opt/rebar stays a plain copy updated by rsync.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Update `autodeploy.sh` and `rebar-autodeploy.service` to implement the box-adaptation spec: self-bootstrapping mirror clone at `MIRROR_DIR=/var/lib/rebar/mirror`, `rsync -a --delete` with `.env`/`.git`/volume excludes into `/opt/rebar`, corrected `ExecStart` path; and create the described `install-autodeploy.sh` installer.
findings.finding: The committed `autodeploy.sh` and `rebar-autodeploy.service` implement the pre-box-adaptation mechanism (git checkout on DEPLOY_REPO=/opt/rebar/src) rather than the box-adaptation spec (self-bootstrapping mirror clone at MIRROR_DIR=/var/lib/rebar/mirror + rsync --delete with excludes into /opt/rebar). The plan's box-adaptation section explicitly calls these out as changes — but the committed artifacts do not yet implement them. The critical safety guards (rsync excludes for .env, .git, Docker volumes) are specified in the plan text but absent from the committed script; if the old checkout-based script ships instead, a source sync could overwrite infra/compose/.env or lack the --delete semantics the plan relies on for correctness.
findings.location: Box-adaptation section / infra/scripts/autodeploy.sh / infra/systemd/rebar-autodeploy.service
findings.suggested_fix: In `autodeploy.sh`: replace the `DEPLOY_REPO=/opt/rebar/src` / `git checkout` pattern with the mirror-clone + rsync logic from the plan (bootstrap the clone if absent, abort on non-HTTPS remote, `git fetch --prune origin main`, diff against mirror, `rsync -a --delete --exclude '/.git' --exclude 'infra/compose/.env' --exclude '/.deployed_ref' $MIRROR_DIR/ /opt/rebar/`). In `rebar-autodeploy.service`: set `Environment=DEPLOY_REPO=/opt/rebar` and `Environment=MIRROR_DIR=/var/lib/rebar/mirror`, and `ExecStart=/opt/rebar/infra/scripts/autodeploy.sh`. Add `install-autodeploy.sh` mirroring `install-observability.sh` that bootstraps the mirror clone and installs the units with the timer disabled.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reconcile the plan's 'DETECT-ONLY in v1' claim for replication/g2p with `autodeploy.sh` lines 112–118 which auto-apply them: either update the plan to reflect that g2p/replication ARE auto-applied in v1 (and address the failure-mode concerns), or remove the auto-apply from the script and implement detect+signal only.
findings.finding: The plan's Box-adaptation §3 explicitly states 'replication.config, g2p, and refs/meta/config are DETECT-ONLY in v1 — a change is signalled (`AUTODEPLOY_ERROR`), never auto-applied' but `autodeploy.sh` lines 113–118 DO auto-apply replication/g2p changes by calling `materialize-g2p-config.sh`.
findings.location: Box-adaptation §3: 'replication.config, g2p, and refs/meta/config are DETECT-ONLY in v1'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Align autodeploy.sh with the Box-adaptation's v1 scope: replace the `changed "$REPL_PATHS $G2P_PATHS"` auto-apply block (lines 113–119) with a detect-and-signal block that emits `AUTODEPLOY_ERROR` for operator action, or update the plan to reflect that config auto-apply IS in v1 scope and update the safety rationale and ACs accordingly.
findings.finding: autodeploy.sh auto-applies replication/g2p config (lines 113–119) contradicting the Box-adaptation's stated v1 narrowing that replication.config and g2p are 'DETECT-ONLY in v1'.
findings.location: autodeploy.sh lines 113–119; Box-adaptation section 'Apply — review-bot ONLY (v1 narrowed)'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace lines 113-119 with: `if changed "$REPL_PATHS $G2P_PATHS"; then err repl-g2p-manual "replication/g2p config changed in $TARGET — needs manual operator apply (v1 detect-only)"; fi`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reconcile the Box-adaptation mechanism with `autodeploy.sh`: either implement the described `MIRROR_DIR` + `rsync --delete` approach (with `.env`/volume excludes) in the script, or update the plan to accurately describe the actual git-fetch-in-place mechanism and confirm `/opt/rebar/src` is a git checkout (not a plain copy).
findings.finding: The plan's Box-adaptation section describes a separate `MIRROR_DIR=/var/lib/rebar/mirror` regular git clone + `rsync --delete` source-sync mechanism, but `autodeploy.sh` implements none of this: `MIRROR_DIR` is absent from the script, there is no rsync call, and the script instead uses `MIRROR_REMOTE=origin` with git ops (`git fetch`/`git checkout`) directly in `DEPLOY_REPO=/opt/rebar/src`.
findings.location: Box-adaptation section: 'Adapted mechanism §1 (Mirror clone self-bootstrapping)' and '§2 (Source sync = rsync -a --delete)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reconcile autodeploy.sh with the Box-adaptation spec: either implement the described MIRROR_DIR clone + rsync --delete approach (with excludes protecting .env, .git, .deployed_ref), or update the plan to reflect that the git-checkout-in-place approach is the actual mechanism and explain how .env and Docker volumes are protected.
findings.finding: The Box-adaptation's core mechanism (MIRROR_DIR regular git clone + rsync --delete + HTTPS supply-chain guard) is not present in the actual autodeploy.sh; the script still uses the original git checkout approach against DEPLOY_REPO=/opt/rebar/src.
findings.location: Box-adaptation section 'Adapted mechanism' steps 1–2; autodeploy.sh lines 30–32, 97

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Fix the DEPLOY_REPO path and ExecStart in rebar-autodeploy.service (and autodeploy.sh default) to match the actual box layout: the copy lives at `/opt/rebar`, not `/opt/rebar/src`; the script is at `/opt/rebar/infra/scripts/autodeploy.sh`.
findings.finding: DEPLOY_REPO path is inconsistent between the plan's box-adaptation ('DEPLOY_REPO=/opt/rebar'), the actual autodeploy.sh default ('/opt/rebar/src'), and rebar-autodeploy.service ('DEPLOY_REPO=/opt/rebar/src').
findings.location: Box-adaptation 'systemd + installer' section; autodeploy.sh line 30; rebar-autodeploy.service line 22
findings.suggested_fix: In rebar-autodeploy.service: set `Environment=DEPLOY_REPO=/opt/rebar` and `ExecStart=/opt/rebar/infra/scripts/autodeploy.sh`. In autodeploy.sh line 30: change default to `/opt/rebar`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Align the rebar-autodeploy.service ExecStart path and DEPLOY_REPO environment variable with the actual box layout: use `ExecStart=/opt/rebar/infra/scripts/autodeploy.sh` and `Environment=DEPLOY_REPO=/opt/rebar` (repo root), consistent with the plan's own box-fact observation and the 'systemd fix' bullet.
findings.finding: The plan's §Box-adaptation 'systemd fix' bullet states `ExecStart=/opt/rebar/infra/scripts/autodeploy.sh`, but the committed rebar-autodeploy.service has `ExecStart=/opt/rebar/src/infra/scripts/autodeploy.sh` (an `/src/` infix). These two paths are inconsistent with each other and with the plan's own box-fact observation that `/opt/rebar` is the repo root (making `/opt/rebar/infra/scripts/autodeploy.sh` the correct path), meaning the service unit as written would fail to start on the live box.
findings.location: Plan §Box-adaptation > 'systemd + installer' bullet vs infra/systemd/rebar-autodeploy.service line 28
findings.suggested_fix: In rebar-autodeploy.service: change line 28 to `ExecStart=/opt/rebar/infra/scripts/autodeploy.sh` and line 23 to `Environment=DEPLOY_REPO=/opt/rebar`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Implement `infra/scripts/install-autodeploy.sh` (mirroring `install-observability.sh`): self-bootstrap the mirror clone if absent, copy unit files to `/etc/systemd/system/`, run `daemon-reload`, and install the timer **disabled** (not `--now`) so a manual dry-run gates the first live enable.
findings.finding: `install-autodeploy.sh` is described as a new deliverable that 'mirrors `install-observability.sh`' and self-bootstraps the mirror clone + installs units with the timer disabled — but no such file exists in `infra/scripts/` or anywhere in the repo.
findings.location: Box-adaptation section: 'infra/scripts/install-autodeploy.sh (new; mirrors install-observability.sh)'
findings.suggested_fix: Model it directly on `install-observability.sh`: copy `autodeploy.sh` to `/usr/local/bin/`, write the service+timer unit files, `systemctl daemon-reload`, and end with `systemctl enable rebar-autodeploy.timer` (no `--now`) plus a printed instruction for the operator to run the manual dry-run first.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Create `infra/scripts/install-autodeploy.sh` (mirroring `install-observability.sh`) that self-bootstraps the mirror clone, installs the `.service`+`.timer` unit files via `daemon-reload`, and ships the timer DISABLED — or update the plan to explain how unit installation is handled.
findings.finding: infra/scripts/install-autodeploy.sh is named throughout the Box-adaptation section and in the additional ACs but does not exist in the repository.
findings.location: Box-adaptation section: 'infra/scripts/install-autodeploy.sh (new; mirrors install-observability.sh)'; Additional AC: 'install-autodeploy.sh installs units with the timer disabled'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm the bash version on box i-00880b2c7f13527c5 supports `**` arithmetic (bash 5.0+ required); if uncertain, replace `BACKOFF_FACTOR ** (n-1)` with a portable `awk` or Python one-liner (e.g. `awk "BEGIN{print $BACKOFF_BASE * ($BACKOFF_FACTOR ^ ($n-1))"`), or add a `bash --version` guard at script startup that fails fast if < 5.
findings.finding: The backoff computation on autodeploy.sh line 85 uses `$(( BACKOFF_BASE * (BACKOFF_FACTOR ** (n-1)) ))`, which relies on the `**` (exponentiation) operator in bash arithmetic. This operator is only available in bash 5.0+; on bash 4.x (e.g. Amazon Linux 2 ships bash 4.2) it produces a syntax error, silently returning 0 or causing the oneshot to exit non-zero. The plan never states which bash version the box provides, so this capability is asserted but unverified.
findings.location: infra/scripts/autodeploy.sh line 85 / Stability §4 (failure backoff)
findings.suggested_fix: Replace line 85 with: `local wait; wait=$(python3 -c "import sys; b,f,n,c=int(sys.argv[1]),int(sys.argv[2]),int(sys.argv[3]),int(sys.argv[4]); print(min(b*(f**(n-1)),c))" "$BACKOFF_BASE" "$BACKOFF_FACTOR" "$n" "$BACKOFF_CAP")` — Python3 is already a confirmed dependency of the script (used in log/err helpers) and handles large-integer exponentiation portably.

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the first-deploy failure path: when the health check fails and no `:prev` image exists, state the exact fallback action (e.g., keep the pre-build container running by NOT calling `up -d`, log `AUTODEPLOY_ERROR first_deploy_failed`, do NOT advance deployed-sha) and add this case to the Self-heal AC.
findings.finding: The first-deploy failure path is underspecified: Box-adaptation §3 says the `:prev` tag step is 'guarded — skip if none, first-build has no rollback target', but the behavior when the health check FAILS on that first build is not described. With no `:prev` image, there is nothing to roll back to — the plan does not say what the script does (leave the failed container running? bring back whatever was running before the build? abort with error?). This is an edge case that matters for the blast radius guarantee.
findings.location: Box-adaptation §3 (Apply — review-bot ONLY) and Self-heal AC
findings.suggested_fix: Before the build, capture the current running image digest (`docker inspect review-bot --format '{{.Image}}'`). On health-check failure with no `:prev` tag, run `docker compose up -d review-bot` with the captured pre-build image reference as a fallback, or simply skip the `up -d` step so the pre-build container remains running.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Update the 'What' section's 'Apply only what changed' bullet to match the Box-adaptation §3 narrowing: replication/g2p should be listed as detect+signal (not auto-applied) in v1, with an explicit v2 note.
findings.finding: The 'What' section describes replication/g2p as auto-applied ('replication/g2p → re-materialise (autoReload / g2p copy)'), but the 'Box-adaptation' section explicitly narrows v1 to detect-only for those components. The 'What' section was never updated to reflect this narrowing, leaving two contradictory apply-surface definitions in the plan.
findings.location: 'What' section ('Apply only what changed' bullet) vs 'Box-adaptation' §3 ('Apply — review-bot ONLY (v1 narrowed)')
findings.suggested_fix: In the 'What' section replace 'replication/g2p → re-materialise (autoReload / g2p copy)' with 'replication/g2p/refs/meta/config → DETECT-ONLY in v1: change is signalled via AUTODEPLOY_ERROR marker; auto-apply deferred to v2 (see Box-adaptation §3 for rationale).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Revise original AC §1 to remove 'replication.config, g2p' from the auto-apply verification list and replace with a reference to the detect+signal verification (e.g., 'a replication.config or g2p change is detected and an AUTODEPLOY_ERROR signal is emitted, not applied').
findings.finding: Original AC §1 says idempotency and selective re-apply is 'verified for review-bot code, replication.config, g2p separately', implying all three components are auto-applied and independently verified. But the Box-adaptation explicitly scopes v1 auto-apply to review-bot only, making the replication.config and g2p portions of AC §1 orphaned criteria that reference non-existent v1 deliverables.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria §1 vs Box-adaptation additional AC §2
findings.suggested_fix: Change the end of AC §1 from '— verified for review-bot code, replication.config, g2p separately' to '— verified for review-bot code; a replication.config or g2p change emits AUTODEPLOY_ERROR and is NOT auto-applied (detect+signal verified separately per box-adaptation AC §2).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace the `python3 -c json.dumps` subprocess in `log()`/`err()` (autodeploy.sh:52-53) with a pure-bash JSON-safe escaping approach (e.g., `printf '%s' "$msg" | sed 's/\\/\\\\/g; s/"/\\"/g'`) or at minimum document the python3 dependency as a hard prerequisite and add a startup guard (`command -v python3 || { ... exit 1; }`).
findings.finding: autodeploy.sh's log() and err() functions (lines 52-53) fork a python3 subprocess on every invocation purely to JSON-escape a string, spawning ~1440 python3 processes/day (2 per timer fire, 720 fires/day). No other infra script (install-observability.sh, fetch-secrets.sh, compose-up.sh, bootstrap-gerrit-admin.sh) uses python3 for string formatting — this is a novel pattern with no existing call-sites that need it. printf + parameter substitution or a simple bash escape function achieves the same result without the overhead or the undeclared python3 runtime dependency in the critical infra polling path.
findings.location: infra/scripts/autodeploy.sh:52-53 (log() and err() functions)
findings.suggested_fix: Replace with a small bash helper: `json_str() { printf '%s' "$*" | sed 's/\\/\\\\/g; s/"/\\"/g; s/$//' ; }` and use `"$(json_str "$*")"` in log/err — no subprocess fork, no python3 dependency, same output.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify whether `/opt/rebar/.deployed_ref` actually exists on the live box and, if so, add logic to `autodeploy.sh`'s first-run path to seed `deployed-sha` from it (falling back to `origin/main` if absent), or remove the claim from the plan.
findings.finding: The plan's Box-adaptation states 'first run seeds the marker from `/opt/rebar/.deployed_ref` if present' but `autodeploy.sh` does no such seeding — it seeds `deployed-sha` from `$TARGET` (the fetched `origin/main` tip) unconditionally, and there is no `.deployed_ref` file anywhere in the repo.
findings.location: Box-adaptation section §4: 'first run seeds the marker from /opt/rebar/.deployed_ref if present'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete validation mechanism for AC5: specify how the backoff behavior will be demonstrated (e.g., a fault-injection run that observes at least two retry intervals doubling, and a second run with a new SHA that confirms immediate retry reset), or add an explicit unit-test for the backoff/SHA-key logic in `autodeploy.sh`.
findings.finding: AC5 (failure backoff) specifies the desired behavior — capped exponential backoff, SHA-keyed reset, loop never auto-disables — but provides no concrete validation mechanism (no 'proven live', no fault-injection protocol, no unit-test reference), leaving it potentially satisfiable by code inspection alone.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC5 (Failure backoff)
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC5: 'Validated by a controlled fault-injection run: break the health check, observe two consecutive retry intervals (≥60s, ≥120s); then push a new `origin/main` tip and confirm the next attempt fires at base interval (≤60s + jitter), evidenced in the deploy log.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Capture the on-box-timer-vs-GH-Actions decision and the v1 detect-only scope for config changes in an ADR or CLAUDE.md section so the rationale is discoverable in the repo, not only in the ticket.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a significant architectural decision (on-box systemd timer vs GitHub Actions OIDC, v1 auto-apply narrowed to review-bot only) that is reasoned thoroughly in the ticket but no ADR, CLAUDE.md update, or persistent design-doc is mentioned — future contributors will not find this rationale outside the ticket.
findings.location: 'Why' section / 'Box-adaptation' §3 rationale / 'Scope' section
findings.suggested_fix: Add `docs/adr/NNN-autodeploy-on-box-timer.md` covering: (1) why on-box timer over GH Actions OIDC, (2) why v1 narrows auto-apply to review-bot only, (3) v2 preconditions for config auto-apply. Reference it from CLAUDE.md or the infra README.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the health-check spec conflict: pick one definition (liveness `/health` 200 OR end-to-end bot-can-review), state it once canonically, update the Self-heal AC to match (replacing 'gate restored' with the precise observable outcome that the chosen probe verifies).
findings.finding: The health-check specification is contradicted between two sections: Stability §3 requires a 30s END-TO-END check confirming 'bot can actually review, not just process-up', but Box-adaptation §3 narrows it to 'liveness: process up + `/health` 200, not a deep readiness check'. The Self-heal AC retains 'gate restored' language, which implies functional review capability, not just liveness. An implementer writing `autodeploy.sh` faces a direct contradiction and the choice has safety significance (a shallow liveness probe can pass while the LLM-review path is silently broken).
findings.location: Stability §3 vs Box-adaptation §3 (Apply section) and Self-heal AC
findings.suggested_fix: Adopt liveness as the v1 probe (consistent with the box-adaptation rationale) but replace 'gate restored' in the Self-heal AC with 'review-bot container passes `/health` 200 within 30s'; add a note that a deeper functional smoke-test (submit a trivial change and assert LLM-Review vote) is a v2 follow-up, consistent with the staged-rollout philosophy.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Wrap `git fetch` and `docker compose build` with explicit wall-clock timeouts (e.g., `timeout 120s git fetch --prune` and `timeout 600s docker compose build`) and treat a timeout exit as a fetch/build failure, routing it through the existing `AUTODEPLOY_ERROR` alarm path so a stalled operation is observable.
findings.finding: The autodeploy loop can hang indefinitely if `git fetch` or `docker compose build` stalls (slow/unresponsive remote or build daemon) — neither operation has an explicit wall-clock timeout. Because the loop holds the flock, all subsequent 2-min timer fires are silently skipped, effectively freezing auto-deploy without triggering any alarm.
findings.location: Box-adaptation §Adapted mechanism steps 1 and 3; Observability section
findings.suggested_fix: Use GNU `timeout`: `timeout 120 git fetch --prune origin` (2× the timer interval is a safe ceiling for a fetch) and `timeout 900 docker compose build review-bot`. On timeout, emit `AUTODEPLOY_ERROR fetch_timeout` / `build_timeout` via the existing marker mechanism so the CloudWatch alarm fires. This keeps the fix minimal and consistent with the existing error-handling path.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either implement the HTTPS-remote guard in autodeploy.sh (e.g. `git remote get-url "$MIRROR_REMOTE" | grep -q '^https://' || { err remote-not-https ...; exit 1; }`) or remove the supply-chain-guard claim from the plan/Box-adaptation section to avoid an unfulfilled safety assertion.
findings.finding: The plan's Box-adaptation section asserts a supply-chain guard: 'autodeploy self-bootstraps [the mirror clone] and aborts if the remote is not https://' — but the committed autodeploy.sh sets `MIRROR_REMOTE=origin` (a named remote in a pre-existing checkout) and performs no URL validation. The HTTPS-enforcement capability is asserted as a safety property in the plan but is absent from the implementation.
findings.location: Box-adaptation §1 ('supply-chain guard') vs infra/scripts/autodeploy.sh line 32 (`MIRROR_REMOTE="${MIRROR_REMOTE:-origin}"`)
findings.suggested_fix: Add after line 59 (`cd "$DEPLOY_REPO"`): `_remote_url="$(git remote get-url "$MIRROR_REMOTE" 2>/dev/null)"; [[ "$_remote_url" == https://* ]] || { err remote-not-https "MIRROR_REMOTE ($MIRROR_REMOTE) URL is not https://: $_remote_url"; exit 1; }`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete test procedure to the Self-heal AC: specify how the broken-image scenario is constructed (e.g., `docker tag review-bot:latest review-bot:prev && docker build --build-arg BREAK=1 -t review-bot:latest .` with a Dockerfile snippet that makes `/health` return 500) and what log/command output constitutes proof of rollback.
findings.finding: The Self-heal AC says the rollback behavior is 'proven live' but no test procedure for constructing a deliberately-broken bot image is specified in the plan. Without a defined procedure (e.g., inject a bad `CMD` or a failing health endpoint into a test branch), an executor cannot reproduce the proof deterministically — 'proven live' is an assertion without an attached proving command.
findings.location: Self-heal AC and Box-adaptation additional ACs section

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a test procedure for the backoff-reset AC: specify a deterministic in-session test (e.g., manually set `last_attempted_sha` + `failure_count` to simulate a bad-SHA state in the state file, then advance the mirror tip to a new SHA, run the service, and confirm from the deploy log that the retry fired at base-interval rather than the capped interval).
findings.finding: The backoff-reset behavior — 'a NEW `main` SHA resets the backoff (fix-forward deploys promptly)' — has no proving command or in-session test procedure. The plan describes the logic (persist last-attempted-SHA + failure count; a new tip resets) but does not specify how an executor verifies this behavior is correctly implemented and exercised.
findings.location: Failure backoff AC and Stability §4 (SHA-keyed reset)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extract the health-check timeout, backoff parameters, timer interval, and path manifest into named constants (env-var overridable or a dedicated config block at the top of `autodeploy.sh`) so they can be tuned without editing the script logic.
findings.finding: Key tunable thresholds — health-check timeout (30 s), backoff base/factor/cap (60 s / 2 / 15 min), timer interval (2 min), and the path manifest for change-detection — are stated as fixed values with no mention of making them configurable; these are the parameters most likely to require tuning after live deployment on the actual box.
findings.location: 'Stability' §3 (30s timeout), §4 (backoff values), 'What' (2-min timer), 'What' (path manifest)
findings.suggested_fix: At the top of `autodeploy.sh`, declare: `HEALTH_TIMEOUT=${HEALTH_TIMEOUT:-30}`, `BACKOFF_BASE=${BACKOFF_BASE:-60}`, `BACKOFF_CAP=${BACKOFF_CAP:-900}`, `TIMER_INTERVAL` (document in the unit file), and a `REVIEWBOT_PATHS` array — overridable via a drop-in `/etc/rebar/autodeploy.env` loaded by the systemd unit.

findings.finding: The backoff/retry state ('persist last-attempted-SHA + failure count') is described behaviorally but its persistence location (file path, format, owner) is never specified — leaving CREATE, UPDATE concurrency, and RETIRE paths undefined for this shared mutable state.
findings.location: Stability §4 (Failure handling) and AC 'Failure backoff' bullet

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Document the rsync-exclude contract (what categories of files must be excluded and why) in a comment block in `autodeploy.sh` and note in the contributing guide that any new runtime artifact placed under `/opt/rebar` must be added to the exclude list.
findings.finding: The rsync exclude list is a silent cross-component coupling: any new runtime artifact added under `/opt/rebar` (e.g., a future secrets file, a cache dir) must also be manually added to the autodeploy exclude list or it will be overwritten on the next sync — this maintenance dependency is not acknowledged in the plan.
findings.location: 'Box-adaptation' §2 (rsync command and exclude list)
findings.suggested_fix: Add a prominently commented `RSYNC_EXCLUDES` array at the top of `autodeploy.sh` with a doc comment explaining the contract, and add a note to `CLAUDE.md` or the infra README: 'If you add a runtime artifact under /opt/rebar, update RSYNC_EXCLUDES in autodeploy.sh'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Implement the HTTPS-origin enforcement described in the plan: before any `git fetch`, assert that `git remote get-url "$MIRROR_REMOTE"` starts with `https://`; abort with an `AUTODEPLOY_ERROR` if it does not — or, if using a separate mirror clone (MIRROR_DIR), validate the clone URL at bootstrap time and re-validate on each run.
findings.finding: The plan's box-adaptation section asserts 'autodeploy self-bootstraps [the mirror clone] if absent and aborts if the remote is not https:// (supply-chain guard)' as an explicit security control at the outbound-fetch trust boundary, but the actual autodeploy.sh implements no such URL check: it uses MIRROR_REMOTE='origin' (a git alias, not a URL), never validates the remote URL is https://, and contains no MIRROR_DIR variable or rsync call — leaving the supply-chain guard described in the plan unimplemented.
